REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS TUTOR PERINI CORPORATION, VALVERDE CONSTRUCTION, INC'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 2, 2017. MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPY OF THE MOVING PAPERS AND OPERATIVE COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 501 NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 11, 2017 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS TUTOR PERINI CORPORATION, VALVERDE CONSTRUCTION, INC'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART, AND SUSTAINED IN PART. THE DEMURRER AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS MOOT BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FILED A DISMISSAL AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TRESPASS TO CHATTEL AND FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO SHOW THAT THE TREES, SIDEWALK, MOSAIC, AND IRRIGATION ARE PERSONAL PROPERTY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED (D501)

DISCOVERY 302, RULING ON SUBMITTED MATTER - PLAINTIFF 601 FOURTH STREET OWNER'S ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS BY DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, TO COMPEL RESPONSES BY DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, AND TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED WAS HEARD ON MAY 2, 2018 BY JUDGE PRO TEM ROGER MEAD AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE PRO TEM MEAD NOW ENTERS THE FOLLOWING RULING/ORDER: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE COURT AWARDS MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST SFMTA, IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF, IN THE AMOUNT OF $1500.00. Off Calendar Aug-21-2018 Continued To Feb-11-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer To Verified Complaint
Off Calendar May-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Arthur Khurin, (650)989-5400 for Defendant/Moving party City and County of San Francisco.
Ivo Keller, (415)243-2687 for Plaintiff/Opposing party 601 Fourth Street Owners Associaton.

Argued by Plaintiff. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer To Verified Complaint is GRANTED. First Amended Answer to be filed forthwith. Order signed in Open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests By Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, To Compel Responses By Defendant San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency, And To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar May-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ivo Keller, Esq. of SSL Law Firm LLP, Tel. 415.243.2687, appearing for the plaintiff/moving party 601 Fourth Street Owners Association;
Arthur Khurin, Esq. of Clapp Moroney, Tel. 650.989.5400, appearing for the defendant/opposing party City and County of San Francisco.

Both sides stipulate that the matter can be heard by Judge Pro Tem Roger Mead.

Matter called, argument held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with modifications on Plaintiff 601 Fourth Street Owners Association's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Requests By Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, To Compel Responses By Defendant San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency, And To Have Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted as follows: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's RFP responses do not include adequate statements of compliance (CCP 2031.210(a), 2031.220) and do not identify with particularity any documents or ESI covered by the objections (CCP 2031.240(b)(1)). CCSF's objections other than those based on a claim of privilege are overruled. With respect to any responsive documents withheld from production based on a claim of privilege, CCSF shall provide sufficient factual information for the other parties to evaluate the merits of the claim (CCP 2031.240(c)(1)). CCSF shall serve amended code-complaint responses to RFPs 1, 4, 5, 6, 13, 14, 17, 19, 22, 24, 29 and 32 by May 23, 2018, and complete the production of documents in accordance with its statements of compliance therein by June 6, 2018. CCSF's Response to Form Interrogatories 12.4, 12.7, 15.1 and 17.1 are deficient. By May 23, 2018 CCSF shall serve amended code complaint responses that shall be as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to CCSF permits (CCP 2030.220). Defendant San Francisco Municipal Transit Authority is to provide responses unless they are out of the case. Sanctions are taken under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/RBU)

AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs TUTOR PERINI CORPORATION, VALVERDE CONSTRUCTION, INC's AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED in part, and SUSTAINED in part. The Demurrer as to the First Cause of Action is OVERRULED as moot because Plaintiff filed a dismissal as to the First Cause of Action. The demurrer as to the Fourth Cause of Action for Trespass to Chattel and Fifth Cause of Action for Conversion is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to show that the trees, sidewalk, mosaic, and irrigation are personal property. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)
